performance_overview: Through 17 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull has 7 completed trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). Cumulative since inception: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY -1.7263%, alpha cushion +1.665 pts. Today (2026-06-22) was the 9th consecutive no-entry session — APH and PANW both gapped up to RSI 75+ at the open then sold off all day; neither pulled back to the RSI 55-60 entry zone. SPY -0.43% today, so flat positioning was correct and added +0.43% alpha.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — only consistently profitable bucket; this is the primary target range; enforce hard ceiling at 60 (n=2, sample small but directionally clean)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — all overbought chase entries have lost; hard ceiling, no exceptions
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — skip entirely; this is now a confirmed gap-up trap pattern
overnight_hold_insights: Zero genuine overnight carries across all 17 sessions. Policy unchanged: profitable + trailing-stop-active + price>EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion all required simultaneously. No position has met all six criteria simultaneously. The thin, extended candidate pool continues to prevent qualified entries, let alone overnight candidates.
recent_market_context: Past 5 sessions (2026-06-16 through 2026-06-22): 9 consecutive no-entry days. VIX 17.2 (low, constructive backdrop). SPY EMA-9 > EMA-21 (trending-up regime) but pulled back -0.43% today. Evening scan (2026-06-22 EOD) produced 5 candidates (0.97% pass rate, up from recent 0.4%) — IBKR, MGM, PANW, ANET, FCX. Candidate pool slightly wider but still no 1.30+ RS leaders. Gap-up trap pattern persisted all week: PANW was a no-entry again today (gap-up reversed).
notes_for_next_session:
FOR PREMARKET AGENT (2026-06-23 ~8:30 AM).

BOOK STATE: Flat. Equity $99,938.80. 0 PDT trades today. No open positions. Kill switch NOT triggered.

EVENING WATCHLIST (ranked by score): IBKR, MGM, PANW, ANET, FCX.

#1 IBKR ($96.83, prev_high $97.72): STRONGEST SETUP. RS 1.193, VCP 0.779, only 0.9% from 52w high, vol_dry 0.741 — all comfortably above threshold. If PM move < 2.5%, this is the primary target. Breakout gate: $97.72. Clean setup with no flags.

#2 MGM ($46.57, prev_high $48.07): Highest RS (1.215) and best vol dry-up (0.499) in the group, but 9.7% from 52w high — farther overhead supply than the 8% guideline. Secondary candidate only if IBKR is extended at open.

#3 PANW ($286.45, prev_high $288.68): RS barely passes (1.101). Was a gap-up trap YESTERDAY — opened RSI 75+ and reversed all day. If it gaps again premarket, scratch it immediately. If it opens flat or slightly down, monitor closely for RSI reset.

#4 ANET ($174.63, prev_high $171.82): Close is ABOVE prev_day_high — today was already a breakout attempt. VCP 0.821 and vol_dry 0.877 are borderline. May be extended by open.

#5 FCX ($69.28, prev_high $70.52): Macro copper play. Borderline VCP (0.835) and vol_dry (0.866). Lower conviction — only trade if a clear catalyst (copper prices, China news) supports the move.

GAP RISK PROTOCOL: Any candidate showing PM move > 2.5% is elevated-gap-risk — scratch from active list, RSI will be above 65 at open.

HARD RULES UNCHANGED: RSI 55-60 only. rel_vol >= 1.3. ATR% >= 0.30%. No chasing. Entry window closes 10:30 AM ET.

Cumulative alpha: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY -1.7263% (+1.665 pts). Capital preservation working as designed.
